div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 16px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 20px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: nowrap;

div.div: Transfer sequence — Drone A pulls ROM X from Drone C (different network) · enable_edge = true

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 95px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 1. BOOT — A, B and C each open ONE persistent OUTBOUND mux to the Edge (connect_tls → HELLO → HELLO_ACK with their reflexive NAT address). The Edge marks each edge_online and writes presence to RDS. Drones also register/heartbeat to Overmind over HTTPS.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 185px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 2. AUTHORIZE — A → Overmind: POST /api/devices/{A}/transfers { source=C, asset=ROM X }. Overmind checks RBAC + same-swarm, MINTS a short-lived HMAC token { sid, from=C, to=A, asset, exp } and writes a transfer_sessions row (status = offered).

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 266px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 3. REQUEST — A → Edge (over its mux): TRANSFER_REQUEST { session_id, token, from=C, asset }.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 346px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 4. OFFER — Edge validates the token OFFLINE (shared SECRET_KEY), looks up C's live mux connection, and pushes TRANSFER_OFFER to C. No drone exposed a port; both sides were reached over their own outbound connections.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 413px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
5. TIER SELECTION — A & C try transports best-first (fall through on failure):
• LAN-direct? C.public_ip ≠ A.public_ip → not usable.
• direct-public? C is not port-forwarded → not usable.
• hole-punch & relay below.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 578px; height: 1px; padding-top: 553px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
6a. TIER 3 — HOLE-PUNCH (preferred)
A & C exchange reflexive candidates via Edge SIGNAL (each learned its public ip:port from the STUN reflector). Mutual-confirm UDP punch. On success a reliable-UDP (windowed-ARQ) channel opens.
→ assetfetch FETCH / CHUNK / DONE runs DIRECT A↔C — bytes do NOT touch the Edge. Resume + hash verify.
✓ Edge marks session active → completed.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 578px; height: 1px; padding-top: 553px; margin-left: 662px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
6b. TIER 4 — EDGE RELAY (fallback, if punch fails)
A and C each send RELAY_OPEN; the Edge RelayHub pairs the two legs and sends RELAY_READY. A runs assetfetch over the RelayChannel; the Edge FORWARDS opaque DATA frames between the two TLS legs (per-session bandwidth limit). The Edge cannot read the bytes on the other tiers; relay legs are TLS to the Edge.
✓ Edge marks session active → completed (or aborted on drop).

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 812px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 7. SAME-LAN SHORTCUT — if A instead pulls from B (same LAN), the selector picks TIER 1 LAN-direct immediately: mTLS GET /peer/* to B.local_ip. The Edge is used for presence/coordination but carries NO bytes.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 906px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 8. OBSERVABILITY — the transfer_sessions lifecycle (offered → active → completed / aborted) is visible at GET /api/admin/transfers and the Super-Admin “Peer Transfers” panel, showing the transport used. The device-status job expires stale rows.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 525px; margin-left: 487px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #ffffff;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; background-color: #ffffff; white-space: nowrap;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 525px; margin-left: 793px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #ffffff;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; background-color: #ffffff; white-space: nowrap;
